title: Boat Rental in Walvis Bay: A Budget Guide to Affordable Charter Costs 2026
html:
Exploring Walvis Bay by boat is surprisingly affordable if you choose shared tours or small motorboats, with prices starting around €50 per person. Depart from the Walvis Bay Yacht Club or the main harbour for a day on the Atlantic Ocean. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Walvis Bay or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Walvis Bay?

Shared tours start at €50 per person, while private half-day motorboat rentals cost from €300. For a full-day catamaran sailing trip with lunch, budget €800 to €1,200 for up to ten guests. Crewed yacht charters for a week run from €3,500 to €6,000. You can compare prices and availability for skippered charters in Walvis Bay on the platform.

Where to set off in Walvis Bay

Most rentals depart from the Walvis Bay Waterfront, a modern marina with fuel and provisioning facilities. The Walvis Bay Yacht Club offers moorings and a launching ramp for smaller boats. Pelican Point, a sandy peninsula 15 kilometres north-west, is a popular destination for dolphin and seal sightings. Other departure points include Sandwich Harbour at the southern lagoon and the quieter slipway at Langstrand, a nearby coastal suburb.

Which boat to rent

  1. Shared catamaran tour: ideal for solo travellers, 12 guests maximum, from €55 per person for three hours.
  2. Small motorboat (4-6 persons): flexible for fishing or sightseeing, half-day from €300, no licence needed with a skipper.
  3. Catamaran full-day charter: spacious for up to 12 guests, includes crew and lunch, from €900.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (Azimut 50): perfect for groups of 8, full-day with crew from €1,500. See the Salty Sun Charters charter page for an example of this type.
  5. Kayak or stand-up paddleboard: basic rentals from €20 per hour at the lagoon.

Cost breakdown by boat type

  • Shared dolphin tour: €50, €70 per person, 3, 4 hours, includes light refreshments
  • Half-day motorboat (6m): €300, €450, up to 6 guests, skipper included
  • Full-day catamaran (12m): €800, €1,200, 10, 12 guests, lunch and drinks
  • Crewed yacht (week): €3,500, €6,000, 8 guests, all-inclusive fuel and crew

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Walvis Bay?

For bareboat rental you need a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC), but most charters provide a skipper. Operators confirm exact requirements at booking. The high season runs from October to March when the Benguela Current brings favourable winds and the south-westerly breeze keeps temperatures mild.

Early morning trips from the Walvis Bay Waterfront often encounter the least wind and the best wildlife activity, making it worth setting out before 9am.

At a glance

  • Price range: €50, €6,000 per trip
  • Peak months: October to March
  • Trip length: 3 hours to 7 days
  • Guest capacity: 1, 12 persons
  • Currency accepted: EUR (widely used alongside Namibian dollars)
  • Distance example: Pelican Point is 15 nautical miles from the Waterfront

Questions about boat rental in Walvis Bay

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Walvis Bay?

Bareboat rental is possible only if you hold an ICC or equivalent, and the boat owner may require a sea trial before departure.

What wildlife can I see on a boat trip from Walvis Bay?

Dolphins, seals, flamingos, and pelicans are common, with the Sandwich Harbour lagoon particularly rich in birdlife during summer months.

Is it safe to take children on a boat rental in Walvis Bay?

Operators provide life jackets for all ages, and calm lagoon tours are suitable for young children, while open-ocean trips recommend minimum age 5 years.

How far in advance should I book a boat in Walvis Bay of 2026?

For peak season (October to March), book at least two weeks ahead to secure preferred vessels and times; last-minute bookings are possible outside this period.

Before heading out, compare the full fleet on the BoatHire24 marketplace, where you can also list your own boat and earn income from visitors to Walvis Bay. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace connecting renters and owners across hundreds of destinations.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Walvis Bay and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
